hypothesis: A supervising meta-agent that dynamically partitions the ALP reactor exploration space into subspaces and coordinates a small ensemble of diverse LLM agents with varying prompts and exploration budgets will yield faster, more diverse discovery of novel reaction pathways and reduce path-dependent bias compared with a single-agent baseline under identical time and resource constraints, as evidenced by higher coverage of the reactor-state space and a greater number of unique, reproducible discovery statements.
